problems.title: Create And Delete
problems.description: Create an array with 5 elements and delete element with index value 2. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create an array with 5 elements and delete element with index value 2.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [])
arr.pop(2)

problems.title: Reverse Array
problems.description: Reverse an array. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('u', ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'])

problems.title: Create Reverse Array
problems.description: Create a new array with the reverse of an existing array. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a new array with the reverse of an existing array.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('u', ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'])
arr1 = array(arr.typecode, [])

problems.title: Append Element
problems.description: Append an element to the array. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Append an element to the array.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3])

    arr.append(4)

    print('Output:', arr, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3])
arr.append(4)

problems.title: Insert Element
problems.description: Insert an element at a specific position. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Insert an element at a specific position.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 4])

    arr.insert(2, 3)

    print('Output:', arr, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 4])
arr.insert(2, 3)

problems.title: Remove Element
problems.description: Remove the first occurrence of an element. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Remove the first occurrence of an element.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 2, 4])

    arr.remove(2)

    print('Output:', arr, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 2, 4])
arr.remove(2)

problems.title: Find Index
problems.description: Find the index of the first occurrence of an element. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Find the index of the first occurrence of an element.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

    index = arr.index(3)

    print('Output: Index of the first occurrence of 3 is:', index, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
index = arr.index(3)
examples.output:
Output: Index of the first occurrence of 3 is: 2
----------------------------------------

test_cases.expected_output:
Output: Index of the first occurrence of 3 is: 2
----------------------------------------
test_cases.label: Test 1

problems.title: Convert To List
problems.description: Convert the array to a list. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Convert the array to a list.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

    arr_list = arr.tolist()

    print('Output: Array converted to list:', arr_list, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
arr_list = arr.tolist()

problems.title: Slice Array
problems.description: Slice the array. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

    sliced_arr = arr[1:4]

    print('Output: Sliced array:', sliced_arr, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
sliced_arr = arr[1:4]

problems.title: Count Occurrences
problems.description: Count occurrences of an element. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 2, 4, 2])

    count = arr.count(2)

    print('Output: Number of occurrences of 2:', count, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 2, 4, 2])
count = arr.count(2)

problems.title: Extend Array
problems.description: Extend array by appending elements from another array. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Extend array by appending elements from another array.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr1 = array('i', [1, 2, 3])

    arr2 = array('i', [4, 5, 6])

    arr1.extend(arr2)

    print('Output: Extended array:', arr1, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr1 = array('i', [1, 2, 3])
arr2 = array('i', [4, 5, 6])
arr1.extend(arr2)

problems.title: Create Char Array
problems.description: Create an array of characters. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
char_arr = array('u', ['a', 'b', 'c'])

problems.title: Bytes To String
problems.description: Convert an array of bytes to a string. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Convert an array of bytes to a string.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    byte_arr = array('b', [97, 98, 99])

    byte_str = byte_arr.tobytes().decode('utf-8')

    print('Output: String from bytes array:', byte_str, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
byte_arr = array('b', [97, 98, 99])
byte_str = byte_arr.tobytes().decode('utf-8')
examples.output:
Output: String from bytes array: abc
----------------------------------------

test_cases.expected_output:
Output: String from bytes array: abc
----------------------------------------
test_cases.label: Test 1

problems.title: Copy Array
problems.description: Create a copy of the array. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a copy of the array.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3])

    arr_copy = array(arr.typecode, arr)

    print('Output: Copy of the array:', arr_copy, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3])
arr_copy = array(arr.typecode, arr)

problems.title: Clear Array
problems.description: Clear all elements from the array. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Clear all elements from the array.
problems.solution:
from array import array

def main():
    arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

    arr = array(arr.typecode)

    print('Output: Array after clearing all elements:', arr, end='\n' + '-' * 40)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
arr = array('i', [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
arr = array(arr.typecode)
examples.output:
Output: Array after clearing all elements: array('i')
----------------------------------------

test_cases.expected_output:
Output: Array after clearing all elements: array('i')
----------------------------------------
test_cases.label: Test 1
